prompt: Generate svg code for an image that looks like: a bicycle with a yellow seat and black background. Don't use markdown just give svg code
raw_decoded:
<svg width="400" height="350">
  <!-- Black Background -->
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="bgGrad1" x1="-2%" y1="top" x2="%-98% "y2=top ">
      <stop offset="0px" stop-color="#ffffff"/>
      <stop offset="76.5%" stop-opacity=".9"/>
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- Main Body of the Bicycle -->
  <g transform="translate(200,20)">
    <!-- Seat Frame -->
    <path d="M -10,-5 L 10,-5 M -10,5 L 10,5 Z"
          fill="none" stroke-width="4" style="stroke:#ffccbc;fill:none;" />
    
    <!-- Wheel Base -->
    <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="15" 
            fill="url(#wheel)" opacity=.8/>
            
    <!-- Rim Details -->
    <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="30" 
        stroke-linecap="round" stroke-dasharray="[1]"  
         stroke-width="4" fill="white"/> 

    <!-- Handlebar Detail -->
    <polygon points="-10,-5 -- 10,-5 "
             translate="(-10,-5)"
             rotate="rotateHandleBarAngle()"
             fill="black"/>

    <!-- Pedal Assembly -->
    <polyline points="-10,-5 -- 10,-5 "
              translate="(x,y)=(0,-5)"
              rotation=rotationPedals()
              fill="red"/>


    <!-- Add some small details to make it look more realistic-->
    <ellipse rx="5", ry="10",
           transform="scale(.5,.5)",
           fill="yellow">        
       // This is where you can add any additional elements or effects.
     </ellipse>
   </g>

</svg>

Generate svg code for an image that looks like: a black background with a white and red flower. Don't use markdown just give svg code
raw_decoded:
<svg width="400" height="350">
  <!-- Black Background -->
  <circle cx="200px" cy="175px" r="98px"/>
  
  <!-- Flower Center - Red Circle -->
  <path d="
    M 200px,
      [red] 
        .6 * random(0-3)
          + 1/2 *
            [
              sin(random(-pi-to-pi)) / 2 +
                cos(random(-pi-to-pi))
                  ] /
                    sqrt(
                      (
                        randn()^2+randt()
                          )*
                            ((random()-1)^2+(random()+1)^2)/2+
                              (((sin(rand())*cos(rand())))*((tan(rand())))^(1/2)))
                                ]
                               "
             fill="#ff0000"
           stroke-width="2"/>

  <!-- Petals of the White Outer Ring -->
  <g>
    % For each petal:
    %
    %   Randomly rotate around center.
    %   
    %   Move outward in concentric circles to create petals effect.

    FOR i FROM 0 TO N DO

      // Rotate randomly by angle theta_i between [-π; π]
      
      RADIUS = radius_of_petal[i];
    
      X_OFFSET = CENTER_X;
      Y_OFFSET = CENTER_Y;

      PATH D =
         "M $X_{offset}$+$R$*$COS(\theta)$ ; 
               P_$(\theta)+Y_{offset}$
       ";
       
     ENDFOR 

     WHERE:

     CIRCLE_RADIUS = Radius Of The Innermost Circular Segment
     
     INNER_CIRCLES ARE SEPARATED BY ANGULAR GAP OF $\Delta \phi$

     OUTER_LAYERS HAVE INCREASING SPACING BETWEEN THEM AS YOU GO OUTward
    
     THE NUMBER AND SIZE OF PETALS CAN BE CHANGED VIA PARAMETERS SUCH AS 'N', 'INNER_GAP' ETC...
     
</svg>
